expectations.text: Response uses agent-browser open command to navigate
expectations.evidence: Step 1: "agent-browser open http://localhost:3000/login" -- uses the open command to navigate to the login page.

expectations.text: Response uses agent-browser snapshot to identify elements
expectations.evidence: Step 2: "agent-browser snapshot -i" -- snapshots interactive elements and shows example output with refs like '@e1 - textbox "Email"', '@e2 - textbox "Password"', '@e3 - button "Sign In"'.

expectations.text: Response uses agent-browser fill for form inputs
expectations.evidence: Step 3: "agent-browser fill @e1 "admin@mycompany.com"" and Step 4: "agent-browser fill @e2 "Test1234!"" -- uses fill command for both email and password inputs.

expectations.text: Response uses agent-browser click for the sign-in button
expectations.evidence: Step 5: "agent-browser click @e3" -- uses click command for the sign-in button.

expectations.text: Response verifies 'Welcome back' text appears after login
expectations.evidence: Step 6: "agent-browser wait --text "Welcome back"" -- waits for and verifies the 'Welcome back' text appears on the page after login.
